mainHeading: TDS Return Filing in India (Fast & Accurate)

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) is a mechanism introduced by the Income Tax Department under the Income Tax Act, 1961 to collect tax at the source of income. Enterprises (also applies to individuals) which make payments to specified classes of recipients (e.g., salary earners) have to deduct tax at respectively applicable rates and deposit the same with the Government. TDS returns must be filed quarterly and contain details such as TDS deducted, challans paid, PAN of deductees and the nature of payments. Correct and timely filing leads to correct tax credit for deductees and help to avoid penalties for deductor. Professionals Law has designed complete TDS filing solutions right from generating the challan to e-filing with validation and compliance under all the provisions of the Income-Tax Department.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Filing of TDS return is required in order to follow tax laws and also credit the TDS to a payee’s account. It can be considered as proof of payment of tax and creates transparency in the relationship between the payer and the payee, persons/sellers and the income tax department. Failure to file or with incorrect information, can subject individuals to interest, penalties and attention from the law. All deductor, whether corporates or government or other are liable to file TDS return including private and government deductor even if the TDS is NIL. Due to TDS, many have considered it waste of time but let me tell you that accurate and timely TDS return filing will save you from penalties and you will have an impeccable record of compliance.

body.title: Legal Compliance Assurance
body.description: Regular filing of TDS returns ensures compliance with income tax laws and prevents notices or scrutiny from tax authorities.

body.title: Avoidance of Penalties
body.description: Timely and correct return filing prevents interest, late fees and other penalties imposed for non-compliance or incorrect data.

body.title: Ensures PAN-Aadhaar Linking
body.description: Accurate TDS returns ensure credit reflects in Form 26AS of the deductee, aiding their ITR filing and tax reconciliation.

body.title: Professional Record Maintenance
body.description: TDS returns contribute to organized financial documentation, which is essential for audits, due diligence and loan approvals.

body.title: Improved Stakeholder Confidence
body.description: Businesses that comply with TDS norms build trust with employees, vendors and authorities.

criteria: Any individual, firm, company, LLP, trust or HUF deducting TDS must file returns.
Companies making payments like rent, professional fees, interest, etc.
Banks deducting TDS on FD interest.
Thresholds and rates vary as per nature of payment.

documents: Nature of payment (e.g., salary, rent, commission)

body.description: Professionals Law gathers all relevant payment and deduction data.
Correct TDS rates and thresholds are applied.
Details are compiled in Form 24Q, 26Q, 27Q or 27EQ as applicable.
The file is validated using File Validation Utility (FVU).
TDS return is uploaded on the TRACES or NSDL portal.
Challans are linked and cross-verified.
Acknowledgment receipt is downloaded and shared with the client.
Professionals Law assists in TDS correction returns, Form 16 generation and TRACES compliance.

body.title: Late Filing Penalty (Section 234E)
body.description: ₹200/day until the return is filed (maximum equal to TDS amount).

body.title: Penalty for Non-Filing (Section 271H)
body.description: ₹10,000 to ₹1,00,000 for non-filing or incorrect filing.

body.title: Interest on Late Payment
body.description:
1% per month for late deduction
1.5% per month for late deposit

body.title: Q1
body.description:
April - June
31st July

body.title: Q2
body.description:
July - September
31st October

body.title: Q3
body.description:
October - December
31st January

body.title: Q4
body.description:
January - March
31st May

body.question: Who is required to file TDS returns?
body.answer: Any individual or organization deducting TDS is required to file quarterly returns.

body.question: What are the different types of TDS returns?
body.answer:
Form 24Q (Salaries)
Form 26Q (Other payments)
Form 27Q (Non-residents)
Form 27EQ (TCS returns)

body.question: Can TDS returns be revised?
body.answer: Yes, correction returns can be filed for updating PAN, challan details or deduction amounts.

body.question: Is TAN different from PAN?
body.answer: Yes. TAN (Tax Deduction and Collection Account Number) is mandatory for filing TDS returns and is separate from PAN.

body.question: What happens if TDS is deducted but not deposited?
body.answer: It may attract interest, penalties and prosecution under the Income Tax Act.

body.question: Can Professionals Law file TDS returns for multiple branches or locations?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law can handle centralized TDS filing for multi-location entities.

body.question: What is the penalty for missing TDS return deadlines?
body.answer: A late fee of ₹200/day and additional penalties may apply for delayed or non-filing.

body.question: How do deductees check their TDS credit?
body.answer: Through Form 26AS available on the Income Tax portal or via TRACES.

body.question: What support does Professionals Law offer post-filing?
body.answer: Professionals Law assists with correction returns, Form 16 issuance, notice responses and compliance audits.
